Writing/chocolate-chip-cookies.title: My Favorite Chocolate Chip Cookies
Writing/chocolate-chip-cookies.content:
The Best Brown Butter Chocolate Chip Cookies
This is my go-to cookie recipe. The secret is browning the butter until it develops a deep, toffee-like aroma, and letting the dough rest overnight in the fridge.
Ingredients

1 cup unsalted butter (browned)
1 cup dark brown sugar
½ cup granulated sugar
2 tsp vanilla extract
2 large eggs
2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour
1 tsp baking soda
½ tsp sea salt
2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips/chunks

Instructions

Brown the butter: Melt the butter in a pan over medium heat. Keep swirling it until it foams and you see brown specks forming. Remove from heat immediately and let it cool for 15 minutes.
Mix the browned butter with the brown and white sugars.
Whisk in the eggs and vanilla until the mixture lightens.
Fold in the dry ingredients (flour, baking soda, salt) just until combined.
Add the chocolate chips.
Important: Let the dough chill in the fridge overnight.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 10-12 minutes.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt while hot!

Enjoy!

Writing/hello-world.title: Hello, World!
Writing/hello-world.content:
Hello, World!
Welcome to my new blog section. This entire HTML page is actually rendered by fetching a static markdown file and parsing it dynamically in your browser.
No build steps. No Webpack. No Node Modules.
Why do this?
Because sometimes simplicity is best. You can just drop markdown files into a folder and they magically appear on the site thanks to standard HTTP fetch and URL routing logic.
// Simple magic logic that parses this!
fetch('post.md')
  .then(res => res.text())
  .then(text => document.body.innerHTML = marked.parse(text));
There is an enormous benefit to being able to completely host an entire website over plain old GitHub Pages using index.html and purely relative links. And it loads blazingly fast!
